mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all FEDFUNDS decline periods over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances or the predictive t-statistic for mean return exceeds 0.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations of RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns with Rank IC below 0.01 or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals supply-side contraction that immediately pressures equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods show positive mean forward returns or out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.005 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases correlate with negative forward returns or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 240 are followed by positive forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases or the predictive t-statistic remains below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Weak quarterly GDP growth combined with declining real auto sales confirms a broad demand shortfall, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns with mean forward return above 0 or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals improving capacity utilization and corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during recovery periods yields negative mean forward returns or out-of-sample predictive accuracy falls below 52 percent over rolling evaluation windows.
